title: CO-151 Denial Code: Prior Authorization Required — How to Respond
meta_description: CO-151 means your claim was denied because prior authorization was not obtained. Learn your rights and how to appeal or get retroactive approval.
target_problem: Patient's claim was denied with CO-151 because prior authorization was not obtained before the service.
body:
CO-151 indicates your claim was denied because the service required prior authorization that was not obtained before the service was rendered.

## What You Can Do

Many CO-151 denials can be reversed by requesting retroactive authorization or filing an appeal. Under federal law, insurers must provide you with a written explanation and an opportunity to appeal any adverse benefit determination.

Steps to take:

1. Contact your insurer immediately to ask if retroactive authorization is available.
2. Ask your provider to submit the prior authorization request with supporting clinical documentation.
3. File a formal internal appeal if retroactive authorization is denied.
4. Document all communications with dates and names.

## Timeline Rules

Most plans require internal appeals to be submitted within 180 days of the denial. Emergency situations may allow expedited review within 72 hours.

## Disclaimer

This content does not constitute legal or medical advice. MyAdvocate is not a law firm. For questions specific to your situation, consult a qualified attorney or healthcare professional.
